channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Japan’s Mogami-Class Frigate Offer to India: A Strategic Technology Bridge, Not a Competition with Indigenous Warships
item.description: Japan’s proposal to jointly manufacture the advanced Mogami-class (30FFM) frigates in India represents a significant inflection point in Tokyo’s evolving defense posture, as well as a nuanced opportunity for New Delhi to plug specific operational gaps without undermining its indigenous naval programs. Contrary to some dismissive reactions from sections of the defense analysis community, the offer is not about replacing India’s highly capable Project 17A Nilgiri-class frigates, but about introducing a complementary capability set that the Indian Navy currently lacks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Integrated Rocket Force: Closing the Capability Gap with China’s PLARF Through One-to-One Missile Matching
item.description: India’s effort to raise an Integrated Rocket Force (IRF) is not an abstract modernization exercise; it is a structured attempt to build a capability matrix that mirrors, and where possible neutralizes, the operational advantages held by China’s People’s Liberation Army Rocket Force (PLARF). Unlike most militaries where missiles are distributed across services, the PLARF functions as a dedicated arm optimized for both conventional and nuclear precision strike, giving China a highly centralized and scalable deterrence and warfighting tool. India’s response is evolving along a different institutional path, but technologically it is increasingly converging toward a one-to-one weapon class matching strategy designed to plug critical gaps across the tactical-to-strategic spectrum.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s entry into European 6th Gen Fighter Program Could Reshape Global Combat Jet Market
item.description: India is actively exploring participation in one of Europe’s sixth-generation fighter jet programs, engaging in discussions with both major consortiums as part of a long-term strategy to strengthen its aerospace capabilities and global defence positioning. The move signals New Delhi’s intent to integrate into next-generation combat aircraft development while leveraging its growing industrial base to influence costs and supply chains.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Taiwan Confirms Indian Support in Hai Kun-Class Submarine Development Effort
item.description: Taiwan’s Indigenous Defense Submarine (IDS) program has emerged as one of the most geopolitically sensitive naval projects in the Indo-Pacific, and recent disclosures from CSBC Corporation confirm that India is among the key behind-the-scenes contributors enabling the effort. The Hai Kun-class (Narwhal) attack submarines, spearheaded by the state-backed shipbuilder in Kaohsiung, are being developed under a carefully constructed “Non-Red Supply Chain” framework aimed at insulating the program from Chinese pressure by leveraging support from a network of trusted international partners.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russia Offers India Two Advanced Su-57 Paths: M1 Stealth Fighter and Twin-Seat Drone Command Variant to Bridge AMCA Gap
item.description: As of April 2026, Russia has significantly broadened its pitch to India around the Su-57 “Felon,” reframing the offer into two distinct configuration pathways tailored to the Indian Air Force’s (IAF) immediate operational gaps and long-term doctrinal evolution. Moving beyond the baseline Su-57E export model, Moscow is now positioning a deeply modernized single-seat variant alongside a futuristic twin-seat configuration aimed at enabling manned-unmanned teaming, effectively reviving elements of the earlier FGFA concept under a more flexible and technology-driven framework.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Redon Systems’ ‘Bheeshan’ Launcher Demonstrates Autonomous Swarm Strike Capability with Achuk-150 Loitering Munitions
item.description: Hyderabad-based Redon Systems has announced the successful trial of its Bheeshan HMV, a four-barrel munition launcher designed to deploy multiple Achuk-150 loitering munitions in rapid succession, marking a notable advancement in India’s evolving loitering munition and autonomous strike ecosystem. The trials showcased the system’s ability to launch and coordinate precision strikes at stand-off ranges, with the deployed munitions autonomously acquiring, locking onto, and neutralizing designated targets without continuous operator intervention.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Mystery Crash in Bhimber: The Viral Video That Exposed Pakistan’s Hidden Losses in POK
item.description: Last year, a chaotic video from Bhimber in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (POK) exploded across social media platforms, particularly on X, drawing intense scrutiny from India’s OSINT community. Located just a few hundred kilometers from the Line of Control (LoC) near the Nowshera sector on the Indian side of Kashmir, Bhimber suddenly found itself thrust into the spotlight. Residents and security forces were captured frantically searching fields and hillsides, desperately hunting for what they believed was a downed Indian Rafale pilot who had ejected via parachute. Eyewitness accounts circulating at the time fueled the frenzy, with locals claiming to have seen a pilot descending into the area.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Myanmar Air Force delegation visits IAF Guwahati Station
item.description: A 15-member delegation from the Myanmar Air Force visited Air Force Station Guwahati (21–23 Apr 26) on an exchange visit focused on transport operations. Through structured interactions and facility visits, the delegation was briefed on flying operations, maintenance practices and base infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Govt and country-backed army during China 2020 standoff: General Naravane
item.description: Former Indian Army Chief General Manoj Mukund Naravane has formally addressed the political controversy surrounding the 2020 China standoff, firmly denying claims that the military was left without direction during the crisis.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NSA Doval, UK Counterpart Discuss Strengthening Defence And Security Cooperation
item.description: National Security Advisor (NSA) Ajit Doval hosted his British counterpart Jonathan Powell for the annual India-UK Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi on Thursday, holding discussions on advancing the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, including strengthening defence and security cooperation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: All 22 Indian Seafarers On 2 Foreign Ships Targeted By Iranian Forces Safe: Shipping Ministry
item.description: As many as 22 Indian seafarers on board the two foreign-flagged ships, which came under fire from Iran’s military in the Strait of Hormuz during the last 24 hours, are reported to be safe, according to an update issued by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Uninformed’ and in ‘poor test’: India after Trump’s controversial social media repost on immigration
item.description: India on Thursday described as “uninformed” and “inappropriate” a social media repost by US President Donald Trump that referred to India and some other countries as “hellholes” in the context of immigration.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘We remain engaged on this’: MEA on reports Nepal may impose duty on goods
item.description: Amid reports that Nepal may soon impose customs duty on goods carried by people travelling from India to that country, the MEA on Thursday said New Delhi remains engaged with Kathmandu on this development.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISI-linked sleeper cell busted in Noida; 2 arrested over terror plot
item.description: Two men were arrested in Noida on Thursday for allegedly working with Pakistani handlers, including from the ISI, as well as some gangsters and “radical YouTubers” of the neighbouring country. The Uttar Pradesh Anti-Terrorist Squad (ATS) investigating the presence of Pakistani sleeper cells created through social media found that two men, one from Meerut and another from Delhi, were allegedly in direct contact with Pakistani operatives and “planning a major disruptive act”.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India advises nationals not to travel to Iran by air or land
item.description: India on Thursday advised its nationals not to travel to Iran by air or land in view of reports of commencement of some flights between the two countries. The Indian Embassy in Tehran in its latest advisory also said that the “airspace restrictions and operational uncertainties due to regional tensions continue to affect international flight operations to and from Iran”.
item.dc:creator: admin
